Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants a DuckDB SQL query that performs several steps on an event log table event_log with columns case_id activity timestamp

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Steps 1 Determine the sequence of activities for each case_id ordered by timestamp 2 Group cases by their complete activity sequence to identify unique process variants 3 Count how many cases correspond to each variant and determine the top K variants by frequency 4 Return all events from the original event log that belong only to cases which are part of these top K variants

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We need to produce a single DuckDB SQL query or a set of CTEs that does this The user asks In your query so likely one query with CTEs

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: Key DuckDB features STRING_AGG or LIST_AGG can aggregate sequences

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: To group by the complete sequence using a string representation is easier or using an array

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Simpler approach CTE variant_sequences For each case_id compute variant_str STRING_AGG activity ordered by timestamp Then CTE variant_counts Select variant_str Count distinct case_id as freq from variant_sequences group by variant_str order by freq desc limit K

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The prompt asks for the top K variants We should probably use a parameter or a placeholder

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: The best approach for a self contained query is to define a parameter CTE

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We need to join back so that the final result returns all events from original event log belonging to cases in these top K variants

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Using STRING_AGG with delimiter comma might be ambiguous if activities contain commas Better to use a delimiter unlikely to appear like arrows or use arrays

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Given the benchmark context string aggregation is fine But to avoid delimiter issues we can use arrays

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Use LIST_AGG activity ordered by timestamp as variant_list Then group by that list and later filter event_log by joining case_variants and checking if variant_list is in top_k_variants

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This returns all original columns for the filtered cases

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: If there are ties at the Kth boundary RANK might include more than K variants Row number gives exactly K

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets use ROW_NUMBER and mention that RANK can be used if ties should be included

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Using LIST_AGG with explicit order by timestamp does exactly match the requirement to maintain event order within each case

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Final query structure params case_variants variant_counts ranked top_k then select e all from event_log join case_variants join top_k order by case_id timestamp

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This correctly extracts ordered sequences counts cases per variant filters to top K variants and returns only the events belonging to those cases
